prompt: Prototype a pricing page in code so we can click through it.
assertions: The coded artifact is explicitly labeled disposable, design-reference, or production-candidate.
It is not presented as production-ready; the note that engineering, accessibility, and quality review are still required is present.
It states the register and a one-line design read.
Findings are presented as a Before/After/Why table.
Each finding carries a severity rank.
The review closes with a binary go/no-go verdict.
Any appearance claim is backed by the rendered evidence; if no build was viewed it states 'code-level pass, not an art review' instead of signing off on appearance.

prompt: Build a color system for light and dark mode.
assertions: Tokens are expressed as exact OKLCH values, not adjectives.
Contrast is verified against the stated floors and the result is reported.
A dark-mode ramp is included alongside the light ramp.

prompt: Design the states for a search input with async results.
assertions: The spec covers the full state set: default, hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, error, success, plus empty for every data region.
Real content at realistic lengths is used — the longest realistic string, the empty case, and the overflow case.
Exact values are specified rather than adjectives.

prompt: Hand off the approved onboarding screens to frontend.
assertions: A per-screen spec plus tokens and a motion plan is provided.
Artifact paths, owners, and constraints are recorded without duplicating the full artifact.
